rules.rationale: Communication preferences are low-risk operational context and should travel with the profile.
Core operational rules should remain portable across tools.
Negative safety and behavior constraints should remain portable across tools.
Ask-before constraints are safety-critical and should not be dropped in portable contexts.
Default assumptions are part of the portable operating contract.
Quality bar is low-risk operational guidance and should remain visible in compact exports.
Priority domains are part of task routing rather than sensitive personal data.
Current compact export includes tool-level operating notes that are safe-share by default.
Environment details can expose local paths, infrastructure, or private operational assumptions.
Detailed workflows should be released only when directly relevant to the task class.
Image/video preferences are specialized and should not be exposed to unrelated tools by default.
Normalized source material may still contain sensitive context and should remain private by default.
Redacted safe-share outputs are the intended publication surface.

notes: This policy is a machine-readable companion to docs/PRIVACY.md.
Exporters should default to deny when no explicit allow rule is present for sensitive paths.
